free_text: Inelastic scattering of protons from O-16 and the
spin-dependent part of the effective interaction
Sector-focused cyclotron.
The scintillation detectors consisted of NaI(Tl)
crystal (2 in. diam., 0.5 in. thick) optically coupled
to RCA-8053 photomultiplier tubes.
Detal
description of experimental procedure.
Data
for 17.0 and 18.8 MeV.
The measurements were made with gas pressures in the
range of 15-30 in. of mercury, except at the forward
angles where lower gas pressures were used to diminish
the effects of multiple scattering.
The target gas was research-grade natural oxygen of
99.9% purity. Several flushings of the gas cell
before each series of measurements insured that the
total initial impurity of the gas was less than 0.3%
Distorted-wave analysis.
The relative errors in the differential
cross sections for the 0+-3- doublet and the 2- state
are typically 5% for angles greater than 50 degrees
and 10% at small angles.
Uncertainty of 2.5% in the absolute scale.
Uncertainty due to finite geometry
Uncertainty due to the absolute calibration of
the current integration
By CAJAD.
Some corrections in the data-section.
